title: Untitled
content:
# Past Clients + Opportunities

History and context for notable past clients and near-miss opportunities. Useful for case studies, re-engagement, and sales framing.

---

title: PearTree
content:
- **Status:** Former client
- **Service:** Local SEO
- **Retainer:** $1,500/month (~4–6 months)
- **Notes:**
  - Past content mistake on their site damaged trust
  - Later appeared in A&E Fire Protection situation as a web/design-side party
  - If they come up again: assume they'll want tighter review/control over implementation, not direct access
  - Relationship history matters — do not treat as cold

---

title: Berlitz
content:
- **Status:** Former multi-location CTR client + near-miss expansion
- **Service:** CTR work (not full-scope local SEO)
- **Locations delivered:** Warsaw, Mexico City (x4), Colombia (x2), Chile (x1), Vienna
- **Expansion attempt:** Alejandro (South America) — deal nearly set for 12 locations, fell through early 2025
- **Notes:**
  - This was CTR manipulation / CTR improvement work, not standard SEO
  - Source files: `AAA - Local SEO Agency/Client work/Clients/Berlitz/`
  - CTR intake forms exist for Polanco, Warsaw, Salitre Bogotá, Santa Bárbara Bogotá, Santiago Chile
  - Keyword CSVs for Warsaw (Polish + German) and Vienna (German)
  - Best use: case study for multi-location rollout capability + CTR expertise
  - Kevin Lynch (friend) is the connection point for Berlitz

---

title: NASM (National Academy of Sports Medicine)
content:
- **Status:** Strategic near-miss opportunity (never closed)
- **Service:** Lead gen / search-intent targeting
- **Context:** Spending tens of thousands/month on Google Ads in the USA. Conversation included ideas around leveraging search-intent lead data tied to relevant training searches.
- **Presentation:** Slides exist in `AAA - Local SEO Agency/Presentations/NASM Presentation/`
- **Notes:**
  - This was larger and more complex than standard SMB local SEO
  - Longer cycle, higher budget, more enterprise-like
  - Treat as an example of what bigger deals look like, not a template to replicate now

---

title: Kota (London — design/branding agency)
content:
- **Status:** Former CTR client — strong results
- **Service:** CTR / GMB optimization
- **Niche:** Design, branding, digital agency in London
- **Keywords tracked:** design agency, branding agency, digital design agency
- **Results:** Strong CTR performance in London, heatmap improvements documented
- **Files:** `AAA - Local SEO Agency/Client work/Clients/Kota/` — heatmaps, keyword rankings, GMB photos, monthly reports
- **Notes:** Good positive case study for CTR-style work in a competitive market

---

title: DD NYC (New York — design agency)
content:
- **Status:** Former CTR/GMB client — strong results
- **Service:** CTR + GMB optimization
- **Client contact:** Anjelika Kour
- **Niche:** Graphic design, UX/UI, branding, web design
- **Duration:** 6 months (6 monthly heatmap reports on file)
- **Files:** `AAA - Local SEO Agency/Client work/Clients/DD NYC/` — 6 monthly reports (PDFs), GMB insights (Jan 2024), graphics, invoices
- **Notes:** Good case study alongside Kota for CTR/GMB-style wins in service businesses

---

title: Scott the Videographer
content:
- **Status:** Former rev-share project — dropped
- **Service:** VSL creation + lead gen for med spa video packages (~$7,200/day shoot)
- **Model:** Rev-share — Donal generated leads, Scott converted
- **Duration:** ~3 months
- **Outcome:** Leads generated but Scott couldn't convert them. Dropped as waste of time.
- **Lesson:** Avoid rev-share where you do front-end work but don't control the close

---

title: Ethan (Starving Students)
content:
- **See:** `knowledge/clients-archived/starving-students-movers/` for full history
- **Summary:** Multi-location moving company owner. High-D personality. 3-month trial contract on 5 locations, ended without renewal. Rich context file exists.
